result.summary: Codefresh exposes a documented HTTP API at `g.codefresh.io` plus two CLIs: the `codefresh` pipelines CLI and the `cf` GitOps CLI; the documented developer credential is a user or service-account API key.
result.description: Codefresh is a CI/CD and GitOps platform for building, deploying, and managing software delivery workflows. It includes pipeline automation, Kubernetes/GitOps deployment features, and account/integration management capabilities.

codefresh_api_key.label: Codefresh API key
codefresh_api_key.setup: Log in to Codefresh and open [User Settings](https://codefresh.io/docs/docs/administration/user-self-management/user-settings/). In the **API Keys** section, generate a new key, give it a name, select scopes, and copy the resulting token. The API integration guide at [Codefresh API pipeline integration](https://codefresh.io/docs/docs/integrations/codefresh-api/) also points to this flow. For service-account automation, admins can generate a key from [Managing service accounts](https://codefresh.io/docs/docs/administration/account-user-management/service-accounts/).
